Patch Set 1:

The readahead increase step should be figured out by the underlying OSC RPC size. And if a file is multistriped, it should be the minimum RPC size of OSCs where stripes reside.

Due to the difficulties to know what value should be set to this configurable item, it's almost useless in practice.

Patch Set 1:

This setting is specifically added for configurations with all OSCs set to max_pages_per_rpc=1024 (i.e. 4 MiB). And even then it requires administrative action in order to apply changes, so nothing will break or change automatically.

If some readahead code rewrite handles this case in the same way, it can simply remove the setting. However, currently Lustre read performance is below values that can be reached since 4 MB BRW introduction (our performance team reported some 24-33% throughput increase with their tests).

Patch Set 13:

Hm, right. lli_blksize is not always properly initialized, so stat returns a large value. cmp tries to map a whole block and fails with ENOMEM.

Patch Set 13:

Gu,

Ok, now I'm confused.

I think, the point proposed by Jinshan was that I should check if your patch handles the task of 4 MiB readahead and if so we can use your patch instead of mine and possibly include the test case in your patch.

The test case works fine with your patch. I only had to modify an ofd param so that client-OSS communiction allows for 4 MiB BRWs. I don't think you need to use any parts of my patch in yours, except for the test case, maybe.

parents.subject: LU-7925 llite: avoid clearing i_nlink for inodes in use

commit.subject: LU-7140 llite: too few 4 MiB RPCs from readahead
commit.message:
LU-7140 llite: too few 4 MiB RPCs from readahead

Currently, readahead step is set to 1 MiB.

In a setup with 4 MiB multibulk BRW RPCs enabled,
sequential reads generate a mix of 1 MiB and 2 MiB RPCs.
First class RPCs result from the readahead window being
moved by 1 MiB. Second class RPCs result from the readahead
window being moved by 1 MiB and at the same time expanded
by 1 MiB.

The only exception is RPCs generated in the first read
syscall when the readahead window is expanded to cover
the whole requested chunk - these can be 4 MiB RPCs.

This patch calculates the increase step as the largest
value, between OSCs, of the largest RPC size.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Perepechko <andrew.perepechko@seagate.com>
Change-Id: Iad95ecad4c89eac1dbf32e75ace2cee8e07237ed
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-2639
